tips.title: Upgrade to HEPA Filters
tips.description: Use HEPA air filters in your home to capture airborne PFAS-laden dust particles.

tips.title: Wet Cleaning Instead of Sweeping
tips.description: Sweep and mop with damp cloths instead of dry sweeping to prevent dust circulation.

tips.title: Replace Non-Stick Cookware
tips.description: Swap Teflon/PTFE-coated pans for cast iron, stainless steel, or ceramic alternatives.

tips.title: Use Ceramic or Cast Iron
tips.description: Cast iron and ceramic pans are durable, chemical-free, and improve over time with use.

tips.title: NSF-53 Certified Pitcher Filter
tips.description: Affordable pitcher filters certified for PFAS removal (e.g., Aquaphor, Clearly Filtered).

tips.title: Whole-House Carbon Filter
tips.description: Install a carbon filter at your main water line to capture PFAS before it reaches any tap.

tips.title: Filter Water for Kids & Pets
tips.description: Provide filtered water to children and pets who are more vulnerable to PFAS exposure.

tips.title: Check Food Packaging
tips.description: Avoid takeout containers and microwave popcorn bags which are often lined with PFAS.

tips.title: Add Fiber-Rich Foods
tips.description: Increase intake of oats, beans, whole grains, and vegetables to aid natural PFAS elimination.

tips.title: Consider Psyllium Husk Supplements
tips.description: Studies show dietary fiber supplements can accelerate PFAS clearance (consult your doctor).
